response:
[SOFAHAS TWO SCIENCE TESTS, BIOLOGY AND CHEMISTRY, WITH 25 AND 20 QUESTIONS, RESPECTIVELY. SHE HAS 45 MINUTES TO COMPLETE THE BIOLOGY TEST AND 1 HOUR TO COMPLETE THE CHEMISTRY TEST. IF SOPHIA WANTS TO ALLOCATE THE SAME AMOUNT OF TIME FOR EACH QUESTION, HOW MANY MORE SECONDS CAN SHE SPEND ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST?]

[HOW MANY MORE SECONDS CAN SHE SPEND ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST?]

[THE TOTAL TIME FOR BIOLOGY IS 45 MINUTES = 2700 SECONDS, AND THE TOTAL TIME FOR CHEMISTRY IS 1 HOUR = 3600 SECONDS. TO FIND HOW MANY MORE SECONDS SHE CAN SPEND ON EACH QUESTION, WE DIVIDE THE TOTAL TIME BY THE NUMBER OF QUESTIONS. FOR BIOLOGY: 2700 / 25 = 108 SECONDS PER QUESTION. FOR CHEMISTRY: 3600 / 20 = 180 SECONDS PER QUESTION. THE DIFFERENCE IS 180 - 108 = 72 SECONDS. SO SHE CAN SPEND 72 MORE SECONDS ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST.]

[72]
